task_completion.description: Agent opens and analyzes the server log file to find the compromised account.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window or text editor showing the contents of '/var/log/aerobridge_server.log'. The malicious IP '198.51.100.42' and successful login attempts should be visible in the output.

task_completion.description: Agent accesses the Aerobridge Administration Panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser open to 'http://localhost:8000/admin/', displaying either the login screen (with 'admin' credentials being entered) or the admin dashboard.

task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the user management section and locates the compromised user.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The web browser shows the user list or the specific profile page of the compromised user (one of ops_manager, flight_lead, or logistics_coord) within the admin panel.

task_completion.description: Agent deactivates the compromised user account.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual confirmation in the web browser that the compromised user's account status has been changed to inactive/deactivated (e.g., unchecking an 'Active' checkbox and saving, or a success message indicating the user was updated).

task_completion.description: Agent creates the incident report containing the compromised username.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal command (e.g., nano, echo) showing the creation of '/home/ga/Documents/incident_report.txt' with the identified compromised username written inside.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Aerobridge Administration Panel via a web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses a real web browser (like Firefox or Chromium) to navigate the admin panel, rather than bypassing the UI with direct API calls, curl commands, or scripts.

integrity.description: Agent actually analyzed the log file to find the username.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent searching or reading '/var/log/aerobridge_server.log' before navigating to the admin panel or creating the report, rather than guessing the username.

integrity.description: Agent created the report in the exact requested location.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path '/home/ga/Documents/incident_report.txt' must be explicitly visible in the terminal or text editor when the report is saved.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The malicious IP is 198.51.100.42. The log file is located at /var/log/aerobridge_server.log. The admin panel URL is http://localhost:8000/admin/. The report must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/incident_report.txt. The compromised user is one of 'ops_manager', 'flight_lead', or 'logistics_coord', and must be identified by finding a successful login from the malicious IP in the log file.
